key_departures.impact: Franchise icon and all-time leading receiver departs after 12 seasons. Limited to 8 games in 2025 (broken collarbone, hamstring) but still the team's X-receiver and red zone weapon. Bucs retain Chris Godwin, Emeka Egbuka (2025 R1, missed most of year with injury), Jalen McMillan, and Tez Johnson, but none replicates Evans' X-receiver big-body archetype or red zone dominance.

Last remaining CB from Tampa's Super Bowl LV championship team. Career-best 3 INTs, 2 forced fumbles, 9 PDs in 2025. Primary starter opposite Zyon McCollum. Bucs retain McCollum and 2025 Day 2 picks Benjamin Morrison and Jacob Parrish, but lose their most experienced outside CB.

Three-year starter who split duties with Bucky Irving in 2025. Pass-catching specialist and experienced No. 2 back behind Irving. Departed without re-signing; Bucs moved quickly to replace with Gainwell. Impact is mitigated by Gainwell signing.

Signed to a 1-year, $14M deal in 2025. Underperformed massively due to leg injuries — only 2.5 sacks in 13 games. His departure has minimal direct impact given that failure, but the EDGE position remains a critical need. Yaya Diaby led team with just 7.0 sacks in 2025; no other edge had more than 3.0. EDGE is Tampa's biggest remaining need entering the draft.

Franchise legend deciding between return to Bucs or retirement. Led defense with 114 tackles in 2025. Bucs have signed Anzalone to partially account for his departure/retirement. ESPN sources indicate David will decide between returning to Bucs or retiring — not signing elsewhere. Need impact is absorbed by Anzalone signing.

Started 16 games in 2025, career-high 39 tackles. Interior presence on a D-line anchored by Vita Vea. His departure creates a depth hole at IDL. Bucs have Greg Gaines also unsigned. Vita Vea remains but is aging (31).

signings.analysis: 3-year, $30M deal with $20M guaranteed is a legitimate investment at TE — $10M AAV is mid-tier starter money at the position. Otton has back-to-back 59-catch seasons and has started every game he's been healthy over four years. With new OC Zac Robinson (who unlocked Kyle Pitts in Atlanta), Otton's ceiling with this offensive system is higher than ever. $20M guaranteed signals this is their guy through at least 2027. TE was a pre-FA need; this fully resolves it. Bucs still have Payne Durham as a blocker/depth piece.
signings.notes: Re-signing of own UFA. Deal reported March 9, 2026 by Adam Schefter and Ari Meirov. Contract terms confirmed by Spotrac.

signings.analysis: 2-year, $17M deal at mid-tier LB starter money. Anzalone is a significant upgrade — 95 tackles, 9 PDs, 2.5 sacks, 1 INT in 2025 for Detroit, voted team captain 5 straight years. He addresses the glaring coverage liability at LB (defense was torched over the middle in 2025 under SirVocea Dennis). However, at 31 on a 2-year deal, this is a bridge/starter move rather than a long-term fix. The Bucs' LB room is still unresolved with Lavonte David's retirement decision pending. Partially addresses LB — the position still benefits from a Day 2 draft pick. Notably turned down more money from the NY Giants for Tampa's tax-friendly environment.
signings.notes: Reported by NFL Network's Mike Garafolo and ESPN's Adam Schefter, March 9, 2026. Guarantees not yet publicly confirmed; estimated mid-range based on AAV. Spotrac lists 2yr/$17M.

signings.analysis: 2-year, $14M deal with $10M guaranteed directly replaces Rachaad White. Gainwell was 2025 Steelers team MVP with 1,023 yards from scrimmage and 8 TDs, including 73 receptions on 85 targets — an elite pass-catching profile that fits perfectly alongside Bucky Irving. At $7M AAV with $10M guaranteed, this is legitimate starter-level investment at RB (ranks 22nd at position per Spotrac). The Bucs now have a clear 1-2 punch: Irving as the explosive runner, Gainwell as the versatile pass-catcher/third-down specialist. RB depth concern is resolved. White's departure is a push.
signings.notes: Reported by ESPN's Jeremy Fowler and Jordan Schultz, March 9, 2026. Guarantees of $10M confirmed by Jordan Schultz and Spotrac.

signings.analysis: Veteran minimum re-sign of backup QB. No impact on team needs. Baker Mayfield is firmly the starter. Pure depth/practice squad insurance.
signings.notes: Reported in A to Z Sports tracker, March 9, 2026.

signings.analysis: Routine re-sign of long snapper at near-minimum deal. Special teams continuity maintained. No impact on positional needs.
signings.notes: Reported in A to Z Sports tracker, March 9, 2026.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Mike Evans' departure to SF on a 3yr/$42.4M deal (only $16.3M guaranteed) removes the team's only true X-receiver/boundary specialist. Godwin, Egbuka (missed most of 2025 injured), McMillan, and Tez Johnson are all slot/zone attackers. There is no direct replacement for Evans' big-body, red zone, and deep-threat profile. WR jumps to critical top need — particularly a physical outside WR early in the draft (Bucs pick 15th overall).

Reddick's departure eliminates the only 'name' pass rusher outside of Yaya Diaby (7 sacks in 2025). No other edge rusher had more than 3 sacks on the roster. Bucs tried and failed to land Hendrickson (price too high) on Day 1 of FA. EDGE remains the single biggest defensive need and a prime candidate for pick 15 or a mid-FA signing. Unchanged at Tier 1.

Dean's departure to Pittsburgh removes the most experienced starter from the CB room. The Bucs retain Zyon McCollum and 2025 Day 2 draftees Benjamin Morrison and Jacob Parrish. The young trio has upside but is unproven; Dean's leadership and starting experience is hard to replace. CB remains a Tier 2 need — depth veteran CB via later-round free agency or Day 3 draft pick still needed. Tier holds.

Anzalone signing (2yr/$17M) substantially improves a position that was a critical weakness — SirVocea Dennis and the 2025 LB corps were routinely torched in coverage. Anzalone's elite coverage skills, 95 tackles, and 5-year captaincy reduce this from a crisis need. However, at 31 on a 2-year deal and with David's retirement decision still pending, LB needs one more piece — ideally a younger LB in the middle rounds of the draft. Drops from Tier 1 to Tier 2.

Otton re-sign (3yr/$30M, $20M gtd) fully resolves TE. He's the starter under new OC Zac Robinson, has back-to-back 59-catch seasons, and the guaranteed money signals genuine commitment. Payne Durham provides depth. No draft capital needed here.

Gainwell signing (2yr/$14M, $10M gtd) directly replaces Rachaad White with an arguably superior pass-catching option. Irving-Gainwell is a legitimate starter tandem. $10M guaranteed at RB is a real investment. Need fully addressed; Bucs can pivot all RB draft considerations to late rounds or UDFA.

Logan Hall's departure (unsigned) leaves a hole alongside aging Vita Vea (31). Greg Gaines also remains unsigned. The Bucs have not added any IDL in free agency as of this update. IDL remains a draft need in rounds 2-4, particularly with an eye toward long-term succession at DT.

Dan Feeney and Mike Jordan — who both stepped into starting roles mid-2025 after Ben Bredeson and Cody Mauch went to IR — remain unsigned UFAs. Goedeke is locked in at RT. No IOL additions in free agency yet. Guard depth and competition remain thin; IOL is a mid-round draft priority.

key_additions.headline: Reliable starter re-signed with real guarantees; continuity at TE under new OC Zac Robinson
Elite coverage LB from Detroit's championship defense addresses Tampa's biggest 2025 weakness
2025 Steelers MVP (1,023 yards, 8 TDs, 73 catches) replaces Rachaad White as Irving's backfield partner

key_losses.headline: Hall of Fame WR ends 12-year Bucs career; franchise's all-time leading receiver leaves a massive X-receiver void
Last Super Bowl LV CB departs; career-best 2025 season (3 INTs, 9 PDs) makes this loss sting at an already thin position
One-year flier ends without resolution; 2.5 sacks in 13 games leaves EDGE as Tampa's most critical draft need
Four-year Bucs back departs as expected; Gainwell signing makes this a clean transition
16-game starter and 2022 first-round pick departs, thinning interior D-line depth behind aging Vita Vea

notes: Lavonte David's status is pending — ESPN sources confirm decision is between returning to Bucs or retirement, not signing elsewhere. His decision will affect LB tier further. Rachaad White has not signed elsewhere as of 2026-03-11. The Buccaneers notably did NOT sign an edge rusher in free agency despite EDGE being their biggest defensive need; Trey Hendrickson's asking price was reported as too high. Cap space figures from OverTheCap.com: $19.96M pre-FA (as of March 9), approximately $15.7M post-signings per OTC current roster data. Cap restructures of Wirfs, Goedeke, and Winfield Jr. could generate additional space. Alex Anzalone guarantee amount not yet publicly confirmed; Spotrac lists 2yr/$17M total. Connor Bazelak and Evan Deckers re-sign details from A to Z Sports tracker.
